pattern: Because both lists are already sorted, walking them together with two pointers finds a shared value without ever re-scanning either list.
hints: Both arrays are already sorted, so you never need to search one from the start more than once.
Walk both arrays with a pointer each; whenever the pointed-at values differ, advance whichever pointer sits on the smaller value.
Stop the moment the two pointers land on equal values (that is the smallest shared value); if either pointer runs off the end first, no shared value exists.

edge_inputs.description: a shared value in the middle of both lists
no value appears in both lists
two single-element lists at the maximum value that match
two single-element lists that do not match
heavy duplicates on both sides must not stall the pointer walk
the very first elements already match
the only shared value sits at the very end of both lists
values spanning both extremes of the allowed range

stress.description: two large random sorted lists, usually with no shared value
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums1 = sorted(rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n))
    nums2 = sorted(rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n))
    return [nums1, nums2]

stress.description: large random sorted lists with a guaranteed shared value planted in both
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    common = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    nums1 = sorted([common] + [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n - 1)])
    nums2 = sorted([common] + [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n - 1)])
    return [nums1, nums2]
